paper.title: The VLT-FLAMES Tarantula Survey: Observational evidence for two distinct populations of massive runaway stars in 30 Doradus

review.summary: This A&A Letter identifies and characterizes 23 single and 1 binary O-type runaway stars in the 30 Doradus starburst region using radial-velocity data from the VLT-FLAMES Tarantula Survey (VFTS) and the Tarantula Massive Binary Monitoring (TMBM). The paper contains two internal LaTeX tables: Table 1 (tab: cluster) provides summary statistics of systemic velocities, velocity dispersions, and runaway fractions for different spatial subgroups of the O-star population; Table 2 (tab: RW) is the core observational result listing kinematic (line-of-sight velocity, peculiar velocity), rotational (projected equatorial velocity), spectral, and evolutionary properties (present-day mass and age) for each identified runaway star. The paper also uses Gaia DR3 proper-motion data retrieved from the ARI Heidelberg archive to support the runaway-desert finding. Acknowledgements reference the ESA Gaia mission homepage and the Gaia DPAC consortium website. No data-availability statement or additional external data-archive URLs are provided in the paper.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the systemic radial velocities and 1-sigma velocity dispersions of presumed single O-type stars in 30 Doradus, comparing sample-statistics and Gaussian-fit methods, and reports the observed runaway-star fraction for each spatial subgroup.

source_refs.caption: Systemic velocities and 1\s\ radial-velocity dispersions from the two analysis methods (see text) and runaway (RW) fractions of the single O-star populations in 30~Dor.
source_refs.label: tab: cluster

columns.name: Population
columns.meaning: Spatial subgroup of the presumed single O-type star sample (Overall, NGC 2070, NGC 2060, or Remaining field population)

columns.name: Sample | size
columns.meaning: Number of presumed single O-type stars in the subgroup

columns.name: Sample Stat. | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Systemic velocity and 1-sigma dispersion derived from the unweighted sample mean and kappa-sigma clipping

columns.name: Gaussian fit | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Systemic velocity and 1-sigma dispersion obtained from Gaussian fit to the generalized radial-velocity histograms

columns.name: RW fraction | (%)
columns.meaning: Observed runaway-star fraction in the subgroup, computed using binomial statistics

internal_tables.evidence: Systemic velocities and 1\s\ radial-velocity dispersions from the two analysis methods (see text) and runaway (RW) fractions of the single O-star populations in 30~Dor.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Core observational-result table listing the 23 single and 1 binary O-type runaway stars identified in 30 Doradus, providing their kinematic, rotational, spectral-classification, and evolutionary properties.

source_refs.caption: Properties of the O-type runaway stars detected in 30~Dor. Quoted uncertainties are 1\s\ errorbars for the velocities \citep{SdKdM13} and 95\%\ confidence interval for the present-day evolutionary masses ($M_\mathrm{act}$) and ages ($\tau$) \citep{SSE18}, but for VFTS~661 for which the mass estimates is taken from \citet{MAS20}.
source_refs.label: tab: RW

columns.name: VFTS | ID
columns.meaning: VFTS target identifier of the runaway star

columns.name: Loca- | tion
columns.meaning: Spatial location relative to the main OB associations (Out. = outside NGC 2060 and NGC 2070, 2060 = NGC 2060, 2070 = NGC 2070)

columns.name: v_los | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Line-of-sight radial velocity of the star

columns.name: \delta v_los | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Peculiar line-of-sight velocity relative to the systemic velocity of the O-star population in 30 Doradus

columns.name: \vrot | (\kms)
columns.meaning: Projected equatorial rotational velocity (v_e sin i)

columns.name: Spectral | Classification
columns.meaning: MK spectral classification of the star

columns.name: M_act | (\msun)
columns.meaning: Present-day evolutionary mass with quoted uncertainties (95% confidence interval for most objects)

columns.name: \tau | (Myr)
columns.meaning: Stellar age with quoted uncertainties (95% confidence interval for most objects)

internal_tables.evidence: Properties of the O-type runaway stars detected in 30~Dor. Quoted uncertainties are 1\s\ errorbars for the velocities \citep{SdKdM13} and 95\%\ confidence interval for the present-day evolutionary masses ($M_\mathrm{act}$) and ages ($\tau$) \citep{SSE18}, but for VFTS~661 for which the mass estimates is taken from \citet{MAS20}.
internal_tables.comments: Table continues across a commented-out table break in the LaTeX source (lines 272-274); the complete table ends at line 294.
